title: Practical business writing
description: Block 6 opener — real business memo at high speed
text: To: All staff. From: Operations. Subject: New office hours. Effective June 1, the headquarters will open at 8:00 a.m. and close at 7:00 p.m., Monday through Friday. Remote work remains flexible. Please update your calendars accordingly. Questions? Reply to ops@company.com.

tips.text: From here on the lessons are lifted straight from working life. Every one is something you will actually have to type.
A memo has its own shape - headers, body, signoff. Learn the shape and the words fall into place.

tips.target: To: All staff. From: Operations. Subject: New office hours. Effective June 1, the headquarters will open at 8:00 a.m. and

tips.text: An address like ops@company.com should run as a single motion now, the at-sign and the dots included. No stopping to find them.

tips.target: close at 7:00 p.m., Monday through Friday. Remote work remains flexible. Please update your calendars accordingly. Questions? Reply to ops@company.com.

character_tips.anna: Everything from here is the writing of working days. Practise it well and the real thing holds no surprises. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.maxim: Memos, emails, reports - the plain bread of any job. Type them cleanly and you are trusted with the loaf. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.knopych: Don't dress it up. A memo's job is to be read once and understood. Keep it plain and keep it moving. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.klavochka: This is grown-up correspondence now. Take care with the small marks - they are what make it read as proper. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
